text:
Josh KerrandJakob Ingebrigtsenwill settle a long-standingOlympicrivalry on Tuesday night in thefinals of the men's 1500m.While eight competitors will take to the track in the event, including renowned runners likeUSA's Cole Hocker, Italy's Pietro Arese, and the Netherlands' Stefan Nillessen,all eyes will be on the battlingTeam GBand Norway stars.

The two have repeatedly come up against one another since 2016, but a fierce spat between Kerr and Ingebrigtsen didn't develop until the 2023 World Athletics Championships in Budapest. Despite being an underdog, Scotsman Kerr beat the2020 Tokyo gold medallistin the 1500m with a seasonal best of 3:29.38 to Ingebrigtsen's 3:29.65.

And the defeat didn't sit well with the 23-year-old from Rogaland who attributed sickness to his defeat while claiming that if he was fully fit, he could beat Kerr "blindfolded". He claimed: "I would have beaten him in that race blindfolded," adding: "But it's good that people run better than they have done before."

Kerr, however, later hit back: "I'm like, 'You've won so many races, you ran fantastic all season that you're just on this - you must be surrounded by so many yes-men that you don't realise that you have weaknesses'.

"And I think that was part of his downfall." He went on: "If he doesn't realize that he's got some real major weaknesses, then he will not win the 1500 meter gold medal next year. And you know, I'm OK with that ... I would love for him to be listening to this."

Fans of the sport have questioned whether the bad blood between the two runners is real or just heat of the moment, but Kerr is insistent that the two are "not friends". In a recent interview with The Times, the 26-year-old said: "People are asking, 'Is it real?' or whatever."



He added: "I don't speak to him. I don't speak to the people I don't want to speak to because we're not friends. That's not our relationship. And that's fine. I don't care." The Team GB star also hopes to bury the hatchet once and for all when an Olympic medal is on the line come Tuesday evening.

Speaking afterSunday's semi-final,in which Jakob bested Kerr by 0.08 seconds with a time of 3:32.38, he said: "Fans should expect the most competitive 1500 meter the sport has seen in a long time. There's been a lot of talking words over the last few years. I'm looking to settle that on Tuesday."

IAAFpresident, Seb Coe, has dubbedthe 1500m finala potential "race for the ages", citing how fans of the sport want to see deep-rooted rivalries between athletes. And spectators are certainly in for a treat when a year-long spat culminates in potentialOlympicglory come Tuesday evening.

A highly decorated athlete, Ingebrigtsen's only Olympic gold to date comes in the form of theTokyo Games of 2021- however, the youngster has 27 first=place finishes in total across the World Finals, European Championships, and Diamond League at senior and junior level.

Kerr, meanwhile, has oneOlympicbronze to his name from the same event Ingebrigtsen won three years ago, as well as a World Championship from 2023, a 3000m World Indoor Championship from this year, and a European Junior Championship from 2015.

The men's 1500m Olympic final takes place at 7.50pm on Tuesday (August 6).

title: Inside Josh Kerr and Jakob Ingebrigtsen's tense Olympic rivalry | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Aaron Morris
description: While eight talented runners will take part in Tuesday's 1500m final, all eyes will be on the heated battle between rivals Josh Kerr and Jakob Ingebrigtsen.
